description: Self-upgrade THIS tmux-cli session — rebuild tmux-cli from source and restart this Claude session onto the fresh binary, continuing the conversation (kill-session + start-attach --resume, with bypass-dialog config prewrite).

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by tmux-cli — do not edit. Changes will be overwritten on next tmux-cli start. -->

You are performing a SELF-UPGRADE of the current tmux-cli session: rebuild tmux-cli from source, install it, and restart THIS Claude session onto the fresh binary WITHOUT losing the conversation (so the newly-spawned MCP server picks up the new binary).

**Requires:** running inside a tmux-cli session, a resolvable tmux-cli source dir, and a native terminal (`$DISPLAY` + konsole/gnome-terminal/xterm/…) for `start-attach` to attach.

Follow `.claude/commands/tmux/self-upgrade.xml` EXACTLY as written. Do NOT improvise the restart mechanism — the sequence (build → prewrite bypass config → kill-session → `start-attach --resume <conversation-id>`), the detached-helper requirement, and resuming by the CLAUDE CONVERSATION ID (never the tmux window UUID) are load-bearing: an in-place restart (Ctrl-C does not exit Claude Code) or a wrong resume id strands the conversation in a dead end.
